title: humea epapposa
parts: Humea epapposa has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, roots, and flowers, which contain various bioactive compounds. The leaves of the Humea epapposa plant are rich in flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots of the plant contain alkaloids, including humeaphylline and humeaphyline, which have been reported to have antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The flowers of Humea epapposa contain saponins and phenolic acids, which may contribute to their potential use in traditional medicine for treating various health conditions.
side_effects: Humea epapposa can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant may lead to headaches, dizziness, and even seizures in severe cases. Additionally, Humea epapposa can cause allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, hives, and itching, in some individuals. Improper use of the plant can also lead to liver damage and kidney problems, making it essential to use it under the guidance of a medical professional.
